# Synthesize a Melody

## Lesson Goal

Represent melodies as lists of note-duration pairs.

## What to Notice

A melody becomes data. That data structure is later reused for ground truth.

## Work With the Notebook

- [Open the notebook source](https://github.com/buildLittleWorlds/marimo-audio-self-tutorial/blob/main/notebooks/05_melody_synth/notebook.py)
- [Run the browser notebook](https://buildlittleworlds.github.io/marimo-audio-self-tutorial/notebooks/05_melody_synth/)

Run locally:

```bash
uv run marimo edit notebooks/05_melody_synth/notebook.py
```

## Feedback Lens

Assess whether the student can modify the list intentionally rather than treating it as magic.

## Instructor Notes

Use this lesson as a small checkpoint. Before asking students to add features,
ask them to name the input, the transformation, the output, and the current
limitation. That habit will matter more in later transcription notebooks than
any one line of code.
